figures.caption: TABLE IV EFFECT OF BACKBONE FREEZING AND GATING MECHANISMS ACROSS HAR BENCHMARKS (MEAN ± STD OVER 10 PERMUTATIONS).

Fig. 4. Cross-subject feature channel correlation matrix averaged over all subject pairs in PAMAP2. Entry (c, c′) shows the Pearson correlation between channel c of subject i and channel c′ of subject j, computed using activityclass centroids as paired observations. The strong diagonal (mean ρ = 0.78) indicates that channels preserve their identity across subjects, while the weak off-diagonal (mean ρ ≈ 0) suggests minimal cross-channel mixing. This supports Assumption 1: cross-subject variation is predominantly channel-wise.
TABLE II COMPARISON WITH REPLAY-FREE CONTINUAL LEARNING METHODS (MEAN ± STD OVER 10 PERMUTATIONS). FA: FINAL ACCURACY (%), FM: FORGETTING MEASURE (%), LA: LEARNING ACCURACY (%)

Fig. 5. Per-subject accuracy evolution with our gating approach on PAMAP2. Unlike the baseline in Figure 1, which dropped to 40% on Subject 1 after four subjects, our method retains 69.5% accuracy while achieving 93.8% learning accuracy on new subjects.
